sourceMovie.title: Resident Evil: Vendetta
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Resident Evil: Vendetta (2017) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Vendetta clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Death Island
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Resident Evil: Vendetta clicked for you, Resident Evil: Death Island (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Resident Evil: Vendetta?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Degeneration, Resident Evil: Death Island, Resident Evil: Damnation—each shares DNA with Resident Evil: Vendetta while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Resident Evil: Vendetta?
faq.answer: Resident Evil: Vendetta (2017) is best described as Action, Animation, Adventure, Science Fiction, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Resident Evil: Vendetta part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Resident Evil (Animated) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
